word: H5855 - עַטְרֹות שֹׁופָן - ʻAṭrôwth Shôwphân - at-roth' sho-fawn' - Atroth

sections.paragraph:
Original: עטרות שׁופן

Transliteration: ‛aṭrôth shôphân

Phonetic: at-roth' sho-fawn'

BDB Definition:

Atroth-shophan = "crowns of their rapine"
  1. a town in the territory of Gad

Origin: from the same as H5852 and a name otherwise unused [being from the same as H8226] meaning hidden

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
